what_it_does: The draft/handoff spec for the System Map's visual-encoding grammar: it defines how a file/folder's metadata facets are bound to visual channels (colour, size, texture, border, glow, opacity) via single-sourced 'encoding sets' stored in CV_MEANING.encodings('system-map'), baked into system-map.json by the generator, and projected (not copied) into the map UI.
observations: Names the single home: CV_MEANING.encodings('system-map') in assets/icons/cv-meaning.js, baked into system/system-map.json; the map HTML is a projection.
The one idea: a facet (metadata) -> channel (visual property) binding called an encoding set, offloading recognition to spatial/visual cognition.

Channels table reserves each visual property for a purpose (colour=primary anchor, size=magnitude relative to siblings in same folder, texture=second categorical facet, border=depth/state, glow=liveness only, opacity=de-emphasis).
Facets list: role, ext, roleGroup, links, usedBy, bytes, depth, state — extensible by computing on the node.
Gives a JSONC set schema supporting discrete (values + fallback + optional folderColour) and scale (stops interpolated) kinds.
Distinguishes live sets (role-colour, type-colour, link-heat, the size selectors) from draft:true sets to be built (depth-border, kind-texture, state-glow).
Notes the size SIZERS currently mirror the sets 1:1 and should resolve from the registry once unified.
Specifies the projection: build-system-map.js writes encoding to model.encoding; system-map.html reads ENC and resolves colours from it, falling back to inline tables only if absent.
Closes with a principle gate: writing a colour/size/texture value inside system-map.html is drift — move it into the profile and read it.
standouts: Glow reserved strictly for liveness/attention only — a disciplined channel budget.
Encoding vocabulary is itself a CV_MEANING profile (surface-scoped), so it round-trips to JSON and is authored without code.
Recognition offloaded to visual cognition so reasoning stays free for meaning — an explicit cognitive-ergonomics rationale.
conventions: Single-source / one-home discipline: the map holds references not copies; drift = a literal in the UI.
Extend-by-registration: add a set entry and it appears in the selector with zero UI code.
Draft:true flag marks data+intent-only sets awaiting a channel renderer.
Numbered 'Piece N' framing within the living-codebase-canvas series.
Each channel reserved for a single semantic purpose (channel budgeting).
concerns: Acknowledged transitional duplication: the size SIZERS mirror the sets 1:1 and are not yet unified to resolve from the registry.
notes: A facet-to-visual-channel encoding grammar, single-sourced as a CV_MEANING profile and projected into the System Map, so the codebase canvas's appearance is data not hardcoded UI.
questions: Have the draft channels (depth-border, kind-texture, state-glow) been implemented in the map yet, or are they still data-only?
Has the SIZERS/sets unification noted as pending actually happened in the code?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — declares itself a draft + handoff spec for the visual encoding and delivers the channels, facets, schema, current sets, projection wiring, and extension rules.
apparent_themes: Single-source / projection (UI reflects registry, never parallels it)
Channel budgeting (each visual property reserved for one meaning)
intention_signals: Trying to make the look of the codebase-canvas a single-sourced, data-driven grammar of facet-to-channel bindings so categories and magnitudes read at a glance, and so new encodings appear in the UI by registration rather than by editing the map.
novelty: Treating the visual-encoding vocabulary itself as a surface-scoped CV_MEANING profile, and the deliberate per-channel semantic reservation (glow=liveness only, size=sibling-relative magnitude).
contribution: Contributes the channel/facet vocabulary, the discrete-and-scale encoding-set schema, the live-vs-draft set inventory, and the projection-from-registry wiring that a fused system would keep as its codebase-visualization grammar.
summary_for_embedding: Spec for the System Map's visual-encoding grammar binding file metadata facets to visual channels (colour/size/texture/border/glow/opacity) via single-sourced encoding sets in a CV_MEANING profile, projected into the map.
intention_for_embedding: To make the codebase-canvas's appearance a single-sourced, data-driven facet-to-channel grammar so categories and magnitudes are readable at a glance and extensible by registration.
